task_completion.description: Import the potluck sign-up CSV into the spreadsheet application
task_completion.visual_evidence: Spreadsheet application (LibreOffice Calc) open, displaying the imported CSV data with columns such as Name, Dish, Category, Servings, and Ingredients.
A specific section, cell note, or highlighting that points out duplicate dish types within a category (e.g., identifying the 3 cookie dishes in the Dessert category).
A specific section, cell note, or highlighting that flags allergen concerns (e.g., identifying the 2 dishes containing peanuts/peanut butter).
Cells showing the calculation of total servings (327) and/or the servings per person ratio (~8.2 based on 40 people).
A summary table, list, or chart showing the count of dishes per category (e.g., Appetizer: 3, Main: 3, Side: 3, Dessert: 6).

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Calc GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreOffice Calc interface with its toolbars, menus, and grid, rather than a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Calculations and summaries were done in the spreadsheet, not via terminal scripts
integrity.visual_evidence: The required summaries, flags, and calculations are visible within the spreadsheet cells, not just printed in a terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the spreadsheet to organize the data
integrity.visual_evidence: The layout of the summary and calculations is integrated into the spreadsheet, possibly showing formulas in the formula bar when a cell is selected.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains exactly 15 data rows. The category distribution is: 3 Appetizers, 3 Mains, 3 Sides, and 6 Desserts. Duplicate dishes to identify: 3 Cookie dishes in the Dessert category (Chocolate Chip Cookies, Oatmeal Cookies, Sugar Cookies). Allergen concerns to flag: 2 dishes with peanut/peanut butter (Thai Peanut Noodles, Peanut Butter Brownies). Serving adequacy calculations: Total servings sum to 327, which equates to ~8.2 servings per person for a 40-person event.
